title: Image → document + linked card
description: Read each uploaded image, file what it says as a searchable document, and add a context card pointing back at that document.
hint: Drop images into uploads/, then pick the collection in config.

do.name: note
do.prompt: Look at the image {image} and write a markdown note capturing what it actually says. Transcribe any text, labels, axis titles, table cells and callouts verbatim — this note is the ONLY thing that will be searchable later, because the image itself cannot be. Describe the structure (what kind of chart/diagram/screenshot it is, what it is comparing) and state plainly what a reader should take away. Do not speculate beyond what is visible; if part of the image is unreadable, say so.

do.name: index
do.prompt: Read notes/{image}.md and COPY OUT the one phrase from it that a colleague would type into a search box to find this — the most specific identifier the note actually contains. The term MUST appear verbatim in the note, and must be SHORT — at most a few words, the kind of thing someone types into a search box, never a whole sentence or a finding. Prefer an identifier from the note's title or a heading over one buried in prose — a vision model transcribes headings far more reliably than body text, so a term lifted from prose is more likely to be a misreading. Copy the characters; do not describe what to look for. Example: for a note headed 'CMP PAD CONDITIONER PN-4471' saying 'pad glazing raised defect counts', the term is 'PN-4471' — NOT 'a part number' or 'the heading' (those describe the term instead of being it), and NOT 'pad glazing raised defect counts' (that is a finding, not a lookup key). Also write a two-sentence summary of the note. Reply with a JSON object holding: term (the copied phrase), title (a short display name), summary (the two sentences), and source (set it to exactly notes/{image}.md).

steps.title: File these images into the collection?
